children.heading: Authority to bring civil actions
children.label: a Authority to bring civil actions
i
children.text: Submit a followup emergency notice under .section 11004(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Submit a material safety data sheet or a list under .section 11021(a) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: Complete and submit an inventory form under containing tier I information as described in unless such requirement does not apply by reason of the second sentence of .section 11022(a) of this titlesection 11022(d)(1) of this titlesection 11022(a)(2) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: Complete and submit a toxic chemical release form under .section 11023(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Publish inventory forms under .section 11022(g) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Respond to a petition to add or delete a chemical under within 180 days after receipt of the petition.section 11023(e)(1) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: Publish a toxic chemical release form under 11023(g) of this title.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “section”.1

children.label: iv
children.text: Establish a computer database in accordance with .section 11023(j) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: Promulgate trade secret regulations under .section 11042(c) of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: Render a decision in response to a petition under within 9 months after receipt of the petition.section 11042(d) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: The Administrator, a State Governor, or a State emergency response commission, for failure to provide a mechanism for public availability of information in accordance with .section 11044(a) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: A State Governor or a State emergency response commission for failure to respond to a request for tier II information under within 120 days after the date of receipt of the request.section 11022(e)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Provide notification to the emergency response commission in the State under .section 11002(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Submit a material safety data sheet or a list under .section 11021(a) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: Make available information requested under .section 11021(c) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: Complete and submit an inventory form under containing tier I information unless such requirement does not apply by reason of the second sentence of .section 11022(a) of this titlesection 11022(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Any State emergency response commission or local emergency planning committee may commence a civil action against an owner or operator of a facility for failure to provide information under or for failure to submit tier II information under .section 11003(d) of this titlesection 11022(e)(1) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Any State may commence a civil action against the Administrator for failure to provide information to the State under .section 11042(g) of this title
Any action under subsection (a) against an owner or operator of a facility shall be brought in the district court for the district in which the alleged violation occurred.
Any action under subsection (a) against the Administrator may be brought in the United States District Court for the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Relief
children.label: c Relief
children.text: The district court shall have jurisdiction in actions brought under subsection (a) against an owner or operator of a facility to enforce the requirement concerned and to impose any civil penalty provided for violation of that requirement. The district court shall have jurisdiction in actions brought under subsection (a) against the Administrator to order the Administrator to perform the act or duty concerned.

No action may be commenced under subsection (a)(1)(A) prior to 60 days after the plaintiff has given notice of the alleged violation to the Administrator, the State in which the alleged violation occurs, and the alleged violator. Notice under this paragraph shall be given in such manner as the Administrator shall prescribe by regulation.

No action may be commenced under subsection (a)(1)(B) or (a)(1)(C) prior to 60 days after the date on which the plaintiff gives notice to the Administrator, State Governor, or State emergency response commission (as the case may be) that the plaintiff will commence the action. Notice under this paragraph shall be given in such manner as the Administrator shall prescribe by regulation.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: e Limitation
children.text: No action may be commenced under subsection (a) against an owner or operator of a facility if the Administrator has commenced and is diligently pursuing an administrative order or civil action to enforce the requirement concerned or to impose a civil penalty under this Act with respect to the violation of the requirement.

children.heading: Costs
children.label: f Costs
children.text: The court, in issuing any final order in any action brought pursuant to this section, may award costs of litigation (including reasonable attorney and expert witness fees) to the prevailing or the substantially prevailing party whenever the court determines such an award is appropriate. The court may, if a temporary restraining order or preliminary injunction is sought, require the filing of a bond or equivalent security in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.heading: Other rights
children.label: g Other rights
children.text: Nothing in this section shall restrict or expand any right which any person (or class of persons) may have under any Federal or State statute or common law to seek enforcement of any requirement or to seek any other relief (including relief against the Administrator or a State agency).

children.heading: By the United States
children.label: 1 By the United States
children.text: In any action under this section the United States or the State, or both, if not a party, may intervene as a matter of right.

children.heading: By persons
children.label: 2 By persons
children.text: In any action under this section, any person may intervene as a matter of right when such person has a direct interest which is or may be adversely affected by the action and the disposition of the action may, as a practical matter, impair or impede the person’s ability to protect that interest unless the Administrator or the State shows that the person’s interest is adequately represented by existing parties in the action.
